goal: Implement all remaining coreutils (~25 utilities) organized by shared requirements

milestones.title: Batch A — File Operations (Pure Lean IO)
milestones.objective: Implement file operation utilities that need zero C FFI — use IO.FS and System.FilePath primitives

applets.name: mkdir
applets.title: make directories
applets.description: Create directories using IO.FS.createDirAll (handles -p). Parse -p flag, create each argument path.

applets.name: mv
applets.title: move/rename files
applets.description: Move or rename files using IO.FS.rename. Supports multiple sources with last arg as destination.

applets.name: ls
applets.title: list directory contents
applets.description: List files using System.FilePath.readDir. Support -a (all), -l (long format using /proc or stat).

applets.name: cp
applets.title: copy files
applets.description: Copy files using IO.FS.readBinFile and IO.FS.writeBinFile. Support -r for recursive directory copy, -f for force.

applets.name: touch
applets.title: change file timestamps
applets.description: Create empty file or update access/modification time using IO.FS.Handle.mk with Mode.write.

milestones.batchDescription:
Implement all 5 file operation utilities: mkdir, mv, ls, cp, touch.

All use pure Lean IO primitives — NO C FFI needed.

For each utility:
1. Create Lentils/<name>/Logic.lean with pure functions
2. Create Lentils/<name>/<name>.lean with IO wrapper
3. Register in Lentils.lean and Main.lean

Available Lean APIs:
- IO.FS.createDirAll(path) — mkdir -p
- IO.FS.rename(old, new) — mv
- System.FilePath.readDir(path) — ls
- IO.FS.readBinFile / IO.FS.writeBinFile — cp
- IO.FS.Handle.mk(path, Mode.write) — touch

Read the existing utilities (cat, head, tail, etc.) for reference on the pattern.
After all 5, run: lake build && verify each with --help

milestones.title: Batch B — File Operations (C FFI)
milestones.objective: Implement file operation utilities that need C FFI for system calls
ffiFunctions: lean_coreutils_unlink(path) — wraps unlink(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_rmdir(path) — wraps rmdir(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_symlink(target, linkpath) — wraps symlink(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_link(target, linkpath) — wraps link(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_chmod(path, mode) — wraps chmod(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_chown(path, uid, gid) — wraps chown(2), returns IO Unit
lean_coreutils_chgrp(path, gid) — wraps chgrp(2), returns IO Unit

applets.name: rm
applets.title: remove files
applets.description: Remove files using unlink(2) FFI. Support -r (recursive), -f (force), -d (empty dirs). Parse flags, iterate args, unlink each.

applets.name: rmdir
applets.title: remove empty directories
applets.description: Remove empty directories using rmdir(2) FFI. Each argument is a directory to remove.

applets.name: ln
applets.title: make links
applets.description: Create hard/soft links using link(2)/symlink(2) FFI. Support -s (symbolic), -f (force).

applets.name: chmod
applets.title: change file mode bits
applets.description: Change permissions using chmod(2) FFI. Parse octal mode (e.g., 755) or symbolic mode. Apply to each argument.

milestones.batchDescription:
Implement all 4 file operation utilities: rm, rmdir, ln, chmod.

These need C FFI for system calls. Add to c/coreutils.c:
- lean_coreutils_unlink(path) → unlink(2)
- lean_coreutils_rmdir(path) → rmdir(2)
- lean_coreutils_symlink(target, linkpath) → symlink(2)
- lean_coreutils_link(target, linkpath) → link(2)
- lean_coreutils_chmod(path, mode) → chmod(2)

For each utility:
1. Add FFI to c/coreutils.c (if not already there from previous runs)
2. Create Lentils/<name>/<name>.lean with @[extern] FFI declarations
3. Register in Lentils.lean and Main.lean

After all 4, run: lake build && verify each with --help

milestones.title: Batch C — Process Utilities (fork/exec)
milestones.objective: Implement process-related utilities that need fork/exec infrastructure (follows the env pattern)

applets.name: nice
applets.title: run with modified scheduling priority
applets.description: Run a command with modified niceness using nice(2) + fork/exec. Support -n increment. Follow the env pattern for fork/exec.

applets.name: nohup
applets.title: run immune to hangups
applets.description: Run a command ignoring SIGHUP. Redirect stdout/stderr to nohup.out if needed. Uses fork/exec infrastructure.

applets.name: timeout
applets.title: run with time limit
applets.description: Run a command with a time limit. Fork, set alarm in child, kill if timeout. Parse duration (s/m/h suffix).

milestones.batchDescription:
Implement all 3 process utilities: nice, nohup, timeout.

These need fork/exec infrastructure (identical to the existing env utility).
Use the same pattern as Lentils/Env/.

For each utility:
1. Add needed fork/exec helpers to c/coreutils.c (if not already there)
2. Create Lentils/<name>/<name>.lean with FFI + IO wrapper
3. Register in Lentils.lean and Main.lean

- nice: call nice(2) in child before exec
- nohup: ignore SIGHUP, redirect stdout to nohup.out
- timeout: set alarm before exec, kill child on timeout

After all 3, run: lake build && verify each with --help

milestones.title: Batch D — Text Processing (Pure Lean)
milestones.objective: Implement text processing utilities with pure Lean logic (no FFI)

applets.name: fmt
applets.title: reformat paragraph text
applets.description: Reformat paragraphs by filling and merging lines at a given width (-w). Pure text transformation on stdin.

applets.name: sum
applets.title: checksum and count blocks
applets.description: Compute BSD-style checksum (simple 16-bit sum) and block count. Read file or stdin, output checksum and blocks.

applets.name: cksum
applets.title: CRC checksum and count bytes
applets.description: Compute CRC32 checksum and byte count. Pure Lean CRC32 implementation.

applets.name: factor
applets.title: factor integers
applets.description: Factor integers into prime factors. Pure Lean number theory (trial division up to sqrt(n)).

applets.name: numfmt
applets.title: convert numbers to/from human-readable
applets.description: Convert numbers between human-readable (1K, 2M, 3G) and numeric formats. Pure string/number processing.

applets.name: pr
applets.title: paginate or columnate files
applets.description: Paginate input into pages with headers, or columnate into multiple columns. Pure text transformation.

milestones.batchDescription:
Implement all 6 text processing utilities: fmt, sum, cksum, factor, numfmt, pr.

All are pure text transformations — NO C FFI needed. Read from stdin, transform, write to stdout.

For each utility:
1. Create Lentils/<name>/Logic.lean with pure functions
2. Create Lentils/<name>/<name>.lean with IO wrapper (read stdin via readStdinText)
3. Register in Lentils.lean and Main.lean

- fmt: reflow paragraphs at -w width
- sum: BSD 16-bit checksum
- cksum: CRC32 + byte count
- factor: trial division prime factorization
- numfmt: human-readable number conversion
- pr: paginate/columnate text

After all 6, run: lake build && verify each with --help

milestones.title: Batch E — Date/Time Utilities
ffiFunctions: lean_coreutils_gettimeofday — wraps gettimeofday(2), returns (seconds, microseconds) as two boxed uint64

applets.name: cal
applets.title: display calendar
applets.description: Display a calendar for the current month (or specified month/year). Pure Lean date arithmetic.

applets.name: date
applets.title: print/set system date and time
applets.description: Print the current date/time using gettimeofday FFI + localtime_r, or parse format strings with +fmt.

milestones.batchDescription:
Implement 2 date/time utilities: cal, date.

- cal: pure Lean calendar display. Calculate day of week, month layout.
  Create Lentils/cal/Logic.lean with date arithmetic.
  Create Lentils/cal/cal.lean with IO wrapper.

- date: needs gettimeofday(2) FFI. Add to c/coreutils.c:
  lean_coreutils_gettimeofday → gettimeofday(2), returns (seconds, microseconds)
  Create Lentils/date/date.lean with @[extern] FFI.
  Support +format string (strftime-like).

Register both in Lentils.lean and Main.lean.
After both, run: lake build && verify each with --help

notes: After Batch A, we'll have 49 applets and the basic file ops needed for builds.
After Batch B, we'll have 54 applets with full file manipulation.
After Batch C, we'll have 57 applets with process management.
After Batch D, we'll have 63 applets with text processing.
After Batch E, we'll have 65 applets — approaching full GNU coreutils coverage.
The plan file at .smithers/remaining-plan.json drives the workflow.
Each utility follows the established pattern: Logic.lean (pure) + Name.lean (IO wrapper).
Use open Lentils.Common.IO.Native; readStdinText for reading stdin.
Registration is now a single Applet entry in Main.lean + import in Lentils.lean.
